affordances.name: Disconfirming evidence equality check
affordances.mechanism: When a team is converging on the answer it wanted, force contradictory signals, missing denominators, and failed cases into the same evidence set as visible wins, then revise the recommendation against the full comparison set.

use_when: A team is converging quickly on a favored recommendation before the evidence is fully in.
Dissenting evidence is being reframed as anecdotal, temporary, special-case, or execution noise.
Visible wins, pilots, references, or case studies are present while churned, failed, rejected, or abandoned cases are missing.
do_not_use_when: The answer would merely accuse others of bias without changing the evidence standard or decision test.
There is no favored thesis, sponsor pressure, missing denominator, or disconfirming evidence to inspect.
The team has already named falsifiers, counted failed cases, and documented what would change the recommendation.
The output would relabel ordinary uncertainty as bias rather than improving hypothesis testing.
case_evidence_needed: The favored conclusion, sponsor-backed recommendation, or preferred thesis under review.
The confirming evidence being emphasized and the disconfirming evidence being minimized or excluded.
The missing denominator: failed pilots, churned users, rejected prospects, abandoned trials, dissenting functions, or quiet losses.
The comparison rule that keeps excluded cases from being waved away as exceptions.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to bring disconfirming observations, objections, failed cases, and quiet losses into the same comparison set as visible confirming evidence.
evidence_required: The confirming evidence currently carrying the recommendation.
The contradictory observations or dissenting functions being minimized.
The failed, churned, rejected, or abandoned cases absent from the packet.
The comparison rule that gives those cases equal evidentiary weight.
good_output_shape: Names the confirming evidence and the excluded counter-evidence side by side.
Counts failed cases in the same denominator as visible wins.
Refuses to treat every contradiction as an exception by default.
Explains how the recommendation changes when the missing evidence is included.

diagnostic_questions: Which dissenting signal is being treated as anecdotal or temporary because it threatens the favored answer?
Which visible win only looks persuasive because failed or abandoned cases were not counted with equal weight?
How would the recommendation change if quiet losses entered the same denominator as visible wins?
misuse_guards: Do not use confirmation-bias language to accuse other people while leaving your own evidence standard unchanged.
Do not treat the existence of a favored hypothesis as proof of bias unless contradictory evidence is being filtered, minimized, or excluded.
Do not stop at naming bias; require a falsifier, missing denominator, or challenge process.
Do not confuse real nuance with the exception trap when the exception is actually critical evidence.

source_evidence.source_quote: tendency to continuously filter for and focus on incoming data points that confirm our current beliefs, attitudes, and opinions
Whenever observations contradict your theory, note them down immediately and take them very seriously
Most useful as a lens when** a team is converging too quickly on the answer it wanted before the evidence is fully in.
dissenting evidence is being reclassified as anecdotal
curated set of visible wins while the quiet losses are missing from the packet

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 4 outcome: strong_affordance_record. PR86 narrowed this card to the missing-denominator/equal-weight transaction after splitting sponsor-backed first-falsifier protection into its own affordance.
dropped_material: Did not extract generic bias education, WYSIATI theory, or professional diagnosis examples as separate records.
Did not treat confirmation bias as a productive model; the useful card is a mitigation and evidence-standard intervention.
open_questions: PR29 should compare this with falsifiability and scientific-method cards to ensure each keeps a distinct activation boundary.

affordances.name: First falsifier before sponsor-backed approval
affordances.mechanism: When sponsor preference shifts review from testing a recommendation to defending it, require the packet to name the first observable falsifying signal and where that signal will be protected before approval.

use_when: A sponsor, executive, committee, or high-status stakeholder has signaled a preferred answer before approval.
Contradictory signals are being downgraded into temporary noise, edge cases, execution error, or anecdote.
The decision process is shifting from testing the recommendation to defending the preferred answer.
The case needs an explicit first falsifier before resources, reputation, or authority lock in the recommendation.
do_not_use_when: There is no sponsor-backed preferred answer or approval pressure.
The team has already named the first falsifying signal and the review point that can act on it.
The issue is a missing denominator of failed cases rather than sponsor pressure around approval.
The output would perform falsifiability theater without assigning where the signal will be checked.
case_evidence_needed: The sponsor-backed recommendation or preferred answer seeking approval.
The contradictory signal currently treated as noise, edge case, or execution error.
The first evidence signal that would prove the preferred answer false or require revision.
The review point, metric, or decision gate where that signal will be checked.
Who can raise the falsifier without it being waved away as an exception.

treatment_requirements.description: Before approving the sponsor-backed answer, require the answer to name the first observable falsifier and the decision process that protects that signal from dismissal.
evidence_required: The preferred answer and who is backing it.
The evidence that would falsify or materially weaken it.
The point where that evidence would appear first.
The incentive or authority pressure that might convert the signal into an exception.
good_output_shape: Names the sponsor-backed claim seeking approval.
States the first evidence signal that would prove it wrong.
Defines where the signal will be checked before more commitment.
Prevents sponsor preference from converting falsifiers into edge cases.

diagnostic_questions: If the sponsor's preferred answer were false, what evidence would appear first?
Where in this plan can that signal be raised before approval?
Who has permission to treat that signal as decision-relevant rather than anecdotal?
What would stop the review from downgrading the falsifier into temporary noise?
misuse_guards: Do not use this card when there is no sponsor-backed preferred answer or approval pressure.
Do not stop at naming an abstract falsifier; require the place where it can be observed and acted on.
Do not let authority pressure turn contradictory signals into edge cases by default.
Do not duplicate the missing-denominator card when the live issue is excluded failed cases rather than sponsor-backed defense.

source_evidence.source_quote: Once a senior sponsor signals the preferred answer, the review process quietly shifts from testing the recommendation to defending it
contradictory signals are downgraded into temporary noise, edge cases, or execution error
Name the first falsifier before approval:
If the sponsor's preferred answer were false, what evidence would appear first, and where in this plan have we made sure that signal cannot be waved away as an exception?

review_notes.normalization_note: PR86 split this from disconfirming-evidence equality because sponsor-backed approval requires a different receiver transaction: naming and protecting the first falsifier before authority pressure locks in the recommendation.
dropped_material: Did not convert all falsifiability language into this card; the card activates on sponsor-backed approval pressure.
Did not extract generic red-team process language without a first observable signal and decision gate.
open_questions: Packet stress review should check whether this card and falsifiability remain distinct when both are nominated.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that teams can become good at naming confirmation bias in others while leaving their own evidence standards untouched. A useful affordance must change the evidence test, not assign blame.

source_evidence.source_quote: Teams can become good at naming confirmation bias in others while leaving their own evidence standards, incentives, and sacred assumptions untouched.

absence_records.reason: The source does not support collecting more favorable evidence as an answer. The operational move is to seek and protect disconfirming evidence, count missing failures, and state the falsifier.

source_evidence.source_quote: Which result in this plan looks like proof only because the failed, churned, or quietly abandoned cases were never counted with equal weight?
If the sponsor's preferred answer were false, what evidence would appear first, and where in this plan have we made sure that signal cannot be waved away as an exception?
if the word **‘exception’** repeatedly surfaces, it often serves to mask the presence of disconfirming evidence.
be more interested in getting it right than being right

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 4 source reading found strong operational depth. Extracted one evidence-equality record and preserved absences for blame language and favorable-evidence accumulation.
dropped_material: Cherry-picking, expert diagnosis, ghostwriting, and cognitive-dissonance passages were retained only as background.
No runtime, prompt, lane, or packet-producer changes are implied.
open_questions: Future review should test whether the card improves source-aware packets without becoming a generic bias label.
